description_long: This cowshed features a large, hip-style roof clad in red tiles, providing ample coverage and weather protection. The structure is primarily constructed from light-colored wooden planks, with visible support beams and posts at the corners and along the open side. One side of the building is mostly open, likely serving as an entry or access point for livestock, while the opposite side is fully enclosed. There is a set of rectangular windows on one end, allowing natural light into the interior. The base of the structure is elevated slightly above ground level, supported by concrete footings for stability. The overall design is functional and robust, suitable for agricultural use in a rural or peri-urban setting.
description: A wooden cowshed with a red hip roof, open entry, and side windows, elevated on concrete footings. The structure is sturdy and designed for agricultural use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open entry supported by wooden posts and the expansive red hip roof.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full height of the red roof and the solid wooden wall with no openings.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the fully enclosed wooden wall and the roof's overhang.
description_view_3: Opposite end view featuring two rectangular windows set into the wooden wall beneath the roof.
attribute_door_style: open entryway with wooden posts, no visible hinged or sliding door
attribute_window_presence: two rectangular windows on one end wall
